title: fix: show model slug on mobile
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
## What changed

- Adds the copyable model slug to expanded mobile model cards.
- Places the slug above the description in a subtle monospace code-style surface.
- Reuses the desktop `ModelId` component for consistent copy behavior and accessibility.

## Why

The mobile card showed the display name but hid the exact model identifier users need in API requests. This makes the slug available without adding noise to the collapsed card.

## Checks

- `biome check --write`
- `tsc --noEmit`
- Local frontend/worker build
- Browser verification at 390 × 844

gist.headline: The slug fits in pocket
gist.blurb: Mobile model cards now keep the important little incantation handy. Expand a card, copy the exact model slug, continue summoning pixels and prose.
gist.summary: Expanded model cards on mobile now show the copyable API model slug above the description, reusing the desktop ModelId component for consistent accessible copy behavior.
gist.impact: Mobile users can now find and copy the exact model identifier needed for API requests without leaving the model card.
gist.visual_concept: Polly is in a cozy garden-side hackerspace, holding a tiny phone with an expanded model card. A clearly labeled pixel-code slug sits above a short description, while a small copy button glows; code-plants and flowers reinforce that the precise identifier is now easy to pick up on mobile.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and a tiny developer headset, happily copying a model slug from an expanded phone-sized model card above its description. A retro terminal and code-plants bloom beside her, with lime green (#ecf874) prominent on the screen and garden pots; simple pixel text reads "COPY SLUG". Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, emotionally warm and friendly.
